title: require_non_empty closure in fetch_session adds indirection for two lines
description: client.rs lines 127-133: fetch_session defines a local closure require_non_empty to validate four string fields and then calls it four times. The closure is three lines; the four call sites are four more lines; total cost is seven lines. The equivalent macro-free inline form is four consecutive if-blocks of two lines each, totalling eight lines — essentially the same. The closure does not reduce total lines and adds an indirection layer (the field name is passed as a string literal, losing the compiler's ability to name the offending expression directly). The closure also fires ClientError::InvalidSession with a &'static str message only, while the inline form could interpolate the field value. Suggested rewrite: replace the closure and its four calls with four plain if-guards: if session.api_url.is_empty() { return Err(ClientError::InvalidSession("apiUrl is empty")); }. This is longer by one line but removes the indirection and is immediately legible without tracing the closure.
close_reason: Replaced require_non_empty closure with four inline if-guards in fetch_session
